title: [DOCS REQUEST] Mount secret at different location
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Secrets are mounted at `/run/secrets/secretname`.

However, some container images require having a secrets file at a specific location, e.g. next to the config file.

**Describe the results you received:**

Cannot use Podman Secrets to pass secrets to a container.
For example: koenkk/zigbee2mqtt

**Describe the results you expected:**

Have an option to pass an in-container path for the secret file to be mounted, as if it were a volume.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Fri Oct 21 08:16:35 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: I believe this is already implemented, `--secret mysecret,target=/custom/path`

> I believe this is already implemented, `--secret mysecret,target=/custom/path`

I had no idea.

Perhaps this could be clarified somewhat in the docs?
I didn't understand you could pass a path that way.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2406013/205082869-5dbb19a1-2ea7-4e28-88c0-089fe6f2d1d0.png)
https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html

So for e.g. the Zigbee2MQTT image, I could do the following?
And the file `secret.yaml` would look the same as `zigbee-creds.yaml`?

```bash
podman secret create zigbee-creds /srv/hass/secret/zigbee-creds.yaml
podman run --secret zigbee-creds,target=/app/data/secret.yaml --volume=/srv/hass/zigbee:/app/data:Z {...} docker.io/koenkk/zigbee2mqtt:latest
```

Well well, looks like that works ...
I had no idea.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2406013/205101772-f6fde284-b4d5-4dc2-be1a-50902b8e6746.png)

Thank you for the pointer @ashley-cui
I reopen so that we don't forget updating the docs.
